segments.text: As he passed by, he saw a man blind from birth.
His disciples asked him,
Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: Jesus answered,
This man didn’t sin, nor did his parents, but that the works of God might be revealed in him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I must work the works of him who sent me while it is day. The night is coming, when no one can work.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When he had said this, he spat on the ground, made mud with the saliva, anointed the blind man’s eyes with the mud,
and said to him,
Go, wash in the pool of Siloam
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: (which means
Sent
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ). So he went away, washed, and came back seeing.
Therefore the neighbors and those who saw that he was blind before said,
Isn’t this he who sat and begged?
segments.speaker: Neighbours

segments.text: Others were saying,
It is he.
segments.speaker: Others

segments.text: Still others were saying,
He looks like him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He said,
I am he.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They therefore were asking him,
How were your eyes opened?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered,
A man called Jesus made mud, anointed my eyes, and said to me,‘Go to the pool of Siloam and wash.’So I went away and washed, and I received sight.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then they asked him,
Where is he?
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: He said,
I don’t know.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They brought him who had been blind to the Pharisees.
It was a Sabbath when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.
Again therefore the Pharisees also asked him how he received his sight. He said to them,
He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and I see.
segments.speaker: Again therefore the Pharisees also

segments.text: Some therefore of the Pharisees said,
This man is not from God, because he doesn’t keep the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: Others

segments.text: Others said,
How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?
segments.speaker: Others

segments.text: So there was division among them.
Therefore they asked the blind man again,
What do you say about him, because he opened your eyes?
segments.speaker: Blind Man

segments.text: He said,
He is a prophet.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The Jews therefore didn’t believe concerning him, that he had been blind and had received his sight, until they called the parents of him who had received his sight,
and asked them,
Is this your son, whom you say was born blind? How then does he now see?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: His parents answered them,
We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: but how he now sees, we don’t know; or who opened his eyes, we don’t know. He is of age. Ask him. He will speak for himself.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: His parents said these things because they feared the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that if any man would confess him as Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue.
Therefore his parents said,
He is of age. Ask him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So they called the man who was blind a second time, and said to him,
Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He therefore answered,
I don’t know if he is a sinner. One thing I do know: that though I was blind, now I see.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They said to him again,
What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered them,
I told you already, and you didn’t listen. Why do you want to hear it again? You don’t also want to become his disciples, do you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They insulted him and said,
You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: We know that God has spoken to Moses. But as for this man, we don’t know where he comes from.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The man answered them,
How amazing! You don’t know where he comes from, yet he opened my eyes.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: We know that God doesn’t listen to sinners, but if anyone is a worshiper of God and does his will, he listens to him.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Since the world began it has never been heard of that anyone opened the eyes of someone born blind.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: They answered him,
You were altogether born in sins, and do you teach us?
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Then they threw him out.
Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, and finding him, he said,
Do you believe in the Son of God?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He answered,
Who is he, Lord, that I may believe in him?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
You have both seen him, and it is he who speaks with you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said,
Lord, I believe!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and he worshiped him.
Jesus said,
I came into this world for judgment, that those who don’t see may see; and that those who see may become blind.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those of the Pharisees who were with him heard these things, and said to him,
Are we also blind?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said to them,“If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you say, ‘We see.’ Therefore your sin remains.
